title.en: VEHICLE BRAKE CONTROL SYSTEMS OR PARTS THEREOF; BRAKE CONTROL SYSTEMS OR PARTS THEREOF, IN GENERAL (electrodynamic brake systems for vehicle, in general B60L; brakes per se, i.e. devices where braking effect occurs, including ultimate brake actuators, F16D); ARRANGEMENT OF BRAKING ELEMENTS ON VEHICLES IN GENERAL; PORTABLE DEVICES FOR PREVENTING UNWANTED MOVEMENT OF VEHICLES; VEHICLE MODIFICATIONS TO FACILITATE COOLING OF BRAKES
Arrangements of braking elements, i.e. of those parts where braking effect occurs {specially for vehicles}
{by locking of wheel or transmission rotation}
acting by retarding wheels
acting otherwise than by retarding wheels, e.g. jet action
Portable devices for preventing unwanted movement of vehicles, e.g. chocks
Vehicle modifications to facilitate cooling of brakes
Brake-action initiating means
for personal initiation
for automatic initiation; for initiation not subject to will of driver or passenger {(limiting speed of vehicles other than rail vehicles B60K31/00)}

Arrangements for adjusting wheel-braking force to meet varying vehicular or ground-surface conditions, e.g. limiting or varying distribution of braking force (by changing number of effective brake cylinders in power brake systems B60T17/10)
Using electrical or electronic regulation means to control braking {(detecting or indicating faulty operation B60T8/885)}
responsive to vehicle weight or load, e.g. load distribution ({using electrical circuitry on regulation means B60T8/17; } B60T8/30 takes precedence; responsive to weight and speed condition B60T8/58)
responsive to vehicle inclination or change of direction, e.g. negotiating bends {(using electrical circuitry or regulation means B60T8/17)}
characterised by producing differential braking between front and rear wheels {(using electrical circuitry or regulation means B60T8/17)}

responsive to a speed condition, e.g. acceleration or deceleration ({using electrical circuitry or regulation means B60T8/17} ; B60T8/28 takes precedence; electric devices on electrically propelled vehicles indicating the wheel slip B60L3/10; measuring linear or angular speed per se G01P3/00)
Control or regulation for continuous braking making use of fluid or powdered medium, e.g. for use when descending a long slope
with hydrodynamic brake
with hydrostatic brake

Transmitting braking action from initiating means to ultimate brake actuator without power assistance or drive or where such assistance or drive is irrelevant (the power assistance or drive being essential B60T13/00)
transmitting mechanically
transmitting by fluid means, e.g. hydraulic

Transmitting braking action from initiating means to ultimate brake actuator with power assistance or drive; Brake systems incorporating such transmitting means, e.g. air-pressure brake systems (arrangements for adjusting wheel-braking force to meet varying vehicular or ground-surface conditions B60T8/00; valves incorporated in such systems B60T15/00)
with mechanical assistance or drive {(combined with fluid pressure B60T13/588)}
with fluid assistance, drive, or release
with electrical assistance or drive

Construction arrangement, or operation of valves incorporated in power brake systems and not covered by groups B60T11/00 or B60T13/00 (valve structures responsive to a speed condition B60T8/34; valves in general F16K)
Application and release valves
Component parts, details, or accessories of power brake systems not covered by groups B60T8/00, B60T13/00 or B60T15/00, or presenting other characteristic features (air compressors per se F04)
{Air treatment devices}
Arrangements of pumps or compressors, or control devices therefor

Arrangements of piping, valves in the piping, e.g. cut-off valves, couplings or air hoses (traction couplings involving joints for supply lines, electric circuits, or the like B60D1/62; couplings peculiar to railway vehicles for, or combined with, couplings or connectors for fluid conduits or electric cables B61G5/06; pipes, cut-off valves, couplings, air hoses per se F16C, F16K, F16L)
Applications or arrangements of reservoirs
Brake cylinders other than ultimate actuators (with built-in wear-compensating mechanisms, ultimate actuators F16D)
Safety devices; Monitoring
Particular use of vehicle brake systems; Special systems using also the brakes; Special software modules within the brake system controller
Active or adaptive cruise control system; Distance control
Brake assistants
Hill descent control
Hill holder; Start aid systems on inclined road
Lane monitoring; Lane Keeping Systems
Engine drag compensation
Automatic or semi-automatic parking aid systems
Pre-actuation of braking systems without significant braking effect; Optimizing brake performance by reduction of play between brake pads and brake disc
Electronic locking-differential
Curve braking control, e.g. turn control within ABS control algorithm
